failedAssertions.reason: Network error during chat (send message) doesn't show error UI. Network error during session list API (history page) DOES show error UI. Pattern: errors during 'secondary' operations show error UI, errors during 'primary' session operations (beginSession, sendMessage) don't.

500 on session create (POST /api/sessions) and 500 on chat API don't show error UI. 500 on profile load and 500 on data export DO show error toast. Same pattern as VAL-ERROR-001: MeditationChat component doesn't properly render error view for session/chat operations.

flowReports.notes: Round 6 re-test after fix-meditation-chat-error-state (commit 657c8ed). Pattern confirmed: (1) Toast IS added in sessionStore catch blocks, (2) Toast works for profile load, data export, session list errors (History component catches these), (3) Toast does NOT work for session create and chat API failures - MeditationChat component doesn't show error view, (4) Tests confirm 'Network error during settings load' PASSES because settings component catches the error. The issue is SPECIFIC to MeditationChat not rendering error state for beginSession/sendMessage failures.

systemicIssues.description: MeditationChat component has conditional logic that causes it to fall through to idle view when beginSession() fails. The error view condition check runs BEFORE the component falls through to the idle view, but when status is 'starting' and error is first set (async state update), the component may re-render before the error state propagates properly, causing it to show the welcome view instead of error view.
systemicIssues.impact: VAL-ERROR-001 (network error during chat) and VAL-ERROR-002 (500 on session create, 500 on chat API) cannot pass.
systemicIssues.rootCause: React/Zustand timing issue: when beginSession() fails, set() is called with {status: 'error', error: message}. But in the MeditationChat component, when the useSessionStore hook returns with status='starting' and error=null on first render, the error view condition is FALSE (due to displayError = error || ... short-circuit). Then when the store updates to status='error', the component re-renders but may not properly detect the state change before showing the fallback idle view.

reRunNotes: Round 6 re-test after fix-meditation-chat-error-state. The fix (commit 657c8ed) added error view with explicit check for status==='error'. But the issue persists because: (1) When beginSession() is called, status becomes 'starting', (2) API call fails and catch block calls set({status: 'error', error: message}), (3) useSessionStore() returns new state with error non-null, (4) But the component's displayError calculation might be short-circuiting before the store update fully propagates. OR the real issue is that the error view condition requires status to be one of ['error', 'active', 'streaming', 'starting'] - but when session creation fails, the component stays in 'starting' state which DOES pass the condition, yet the error is still not showing. This suggests the issue is in how React re-renders on Zustand state change - the component might be re-rendering with stale error value from the previous render cycle.
